title: apium ternatum var. ranunculifolium
intro: Apium ternatum var. ranunculifolium, also known as Wild Celery, is a type of wild celery plant that belongs to the Apiaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Apium ternatum var. ranunculifolium is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil for its medicinal properties, with the tea being used to treat various health issues and the infused oil being applied topically for skin and wound care.
